ucs.title: [LLM] MoustachedBouncer ISP-level AitM fake 'Windows Update' lure dropping JScript loader (Disco/NightClub)
ucs.description: Hunts MoustachedBouncer's signature initial-access chain against embassies in Belarus: an ISP-mediated adversary-in-the-middle redirects victims visiting plain-HTTP sites to a spoofed Windows Update / captive-portal page that delivers a JScript or .url payload, which spawns wscript.exe/cscript.exe (and follow-on PowerShell) from an Internet-zone temp path. ESET attributes this exact 'Disco' plug-in delivery pattern uniquely to MoustachedBouncer (G1019).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("iexplore.exe","msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","explorer.exe","outlook.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe") AND (Processes.process IN ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*.js","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*.jse","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*.wsf","*\\Downloads\\*.js","*\\INetCache\\*") OR match(Processes.process,"(?i)(windowsupdate|wuauserv|update\\.microsoft|kb\\d{6,})")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Web.url) as urls values(Web.dest) as web_dest from datamodel=Web where Web.url="*update*" AND NOT (Web.url IN ("*.update.microsoft.com*","*.windowsupdate.com*","*.delivery.mp.microsoft.com*","*.windowsupdate.microsoft.com*")) earliest=-30m by Web.src | rename Web.src as dest] | where isnotnull(urls) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime dest user parent_process_name process_name process urls web_dest
ucs.kql:
let SuspiciousUpdateBrowsing = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any ("windowsupdate","update.microsoft","wuauserv","captive","msftncsi")
| where RemoteUrl !endswith ".windowsupdate.com" and RemoteUrl !endswith ".microsoft.com" and RemoteUrl !endswith ".msftncsi.com"
| where RemotePort in (80, 8080)
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("iexplore.exe","msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","explorer.exe","outlook.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".js",".jse",".wsf",".hta",".url",".lnk")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(windowsupdate|kb\d{6,}|update\.microsoft)"
| where FolderPath has_any ("\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\","\\INetCache\\","\\Downloads\\")
| join kind=inner (SuspiciousUpdateBrowsing) on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (NetTime .. (NetTime + 30m))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName=AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: MoustachedBouncer is the only documented actor running an ISP-side AitM (likely via Belarusian operator lawful-intercept tooling) that redirects HTTP traffic to a counterfeit Windows-Update page in order to drop the 'Disco' framework against embassy hosts. Joining (a) plaintext HTTP requests to update-themed URLs that don't terminate on legitimate Microsoft FQDNs, with (b) a browser/Outlook proces

ucs.title: [LLM] MoustachedBouncer NightClub internal SMB staging of screenshots + internal proxy beacon
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploitation behaviour of the NightClub implant: periodic GDI screen captures written into a hidden staging directory on an internal SMB share (T1074.002) followed by exfil through an internal proxy chain (T1090.001) used to defeat embassy egress filtering. This share-then-proxy pattern, combined with the MoustachedBouncer victimology (foreign missions in Minsk), is highly specific to G1019.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count values(Filesystem.file_path) as paths values(Filesystem.file_name) as files dc(Filesystem.file_name) as file_count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path="\\\\*\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\IPC$\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\C$\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ADMIN$\\*") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.bmp" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.jpg" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.png" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dat" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.bin") AND Filesystem.action="created" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where file_count >= 5 | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as proxy_dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as proxy_ports from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_category="internal" AND All_Traffic.dest_port IN (8080,8888,3128,1080,8443,4443) by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest | where count > 50] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime dest user process_name file_count paths proxy_dest proxy_ports
ucs.kql:
let ScreenshotStaging = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FolderPath startswith "\\\\" or FolderPath has_any ("\\C$\\","\\ADMIN$\\","\\IPC$\\")
| where FileName endswith ".bmp" or FileName endswith ".jpg" or FileName endswith ".png"
     or FileName endswith ".dat" or FileName endswith ".bin"
| summarize FileCount=dcount(FileName), Files=make_set(FileName, 25), Paths=make_set(FolderPath, 10),
            FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where FileCount >= 5;
let InternalProxyBeacon = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| where ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP)
| where RemotePort in (8080, 8888, 3128, 1080, 8443, 4443)
| summarize Connections=count(), DistinctDests=dcount(RemoteIP), Dests=make_set(RemoteIP, 10) by DeviceId, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where Connections > 50;
ScreenshotStaging
| join kind=inner (InternalProxyBeacon) on DeviceId
| project FirstSeen, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileCount, Files, Paths, Connections, Dests
ucs.rationale: ESET's reporting on NightClub describes the implant grabbing screenshots and dropping them into a remote SMB staging location, then funneling collected data out via an internal proxy host inside the embassy LAN — a deliberate design choice to survive air-gap-ish egress restrictions in Minsk. Correlating high-volume bitmap/binary writes to admin shares with the same host opening many sessions to in
